description: Empirical over/underperformance deltas. Based on user observations and collected sentiment.
methodology: Magic factor: some models outperform or underperform their AA benchmark scores in practice. These deltas capture that gap.

deepseek-v4-flash-free.source: User: 'reasoning traces more effective, just gets shit done instead of asking for clarification'
evidence: User 500M+ tokens experience across DeepSeek models
Reasoning traces capture more context than score suggests
NVIDIA Spark user (tonyd615): 'hit-or-miss' for agentic work
deepseek-v4-flash-free.notes: The 'magic' factor — reasoning traces make it feel smarter than 40.3

nemotron-3-ultra-free.source: User: 'gets stuck in clarification loops, lacks magic'
evidence: Higher AA score than DeepSeek but worse in practice
nemotron-3-ultra-free.notes: Score inflated by benchmarks, poor instruction following in practice

minimax-m3-free.source: User: 'haven't used minimax too much yet'
evidence: Highest free model AA score after recalibration
minimax-m3-free.notes: Still highest free model but gap to others narrowed from 8pts to 4pts

glm-5.2.source: User: 'very likely better than minimax by a considerable degree'
evidence: Now IN AA cache — AI 51.1, Coding 68.8
glm-5.2.notes: NOW IN AA CACHE. Highest scoring model. But PAID. Free version unknown.

mimo-v2.5-free.source: User: 'has magic, 500M-1B tokens of usage, really liked it'
evidence: Magic factor present but less than DeepSeek
Weakness: ignores custom tool calling syntax (HN user 'andai')
mimo-v2.5-free.notes: Good general intelligence, weak on tool calling syntax. Nearly tied with DeepSeek now.

evidence: Speed advantage: 53 TPS vs DeepSeek 21 TPS
qwen3.6-plus-free.notes: Speed champion but IQ dropped from 50.0 to 39.6

stepfun.notes: NOW IN AA CACHE. Fast but low IQ. User says overperforms.

gemma-4-31b-it.notes: Underperforms AND dropped in recalibration. Still only free vision option.

notes: Magic factor is real — some models 'just get shit done' while others loop
DeepSeek V4 Flash is the clearest overperformer
Nemotron 3 Ultra is the clearest underperformer
Most models need more empirical data before deltas are reliable
GLM 5.2 and StepFun have NO benchmark data — user claims only
